behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module-to-client handoff: payload construction must survive wiring into the API call path. An in-process fake API client that captures outbound payload is sufficient; no real network/OS/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on model judgment. Rejected higher levels: System/Agentic are disqualified by behavior text focusing on request content (`request sent ... includes ... labels`), not live provider behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module with a fake provider client, invoke classify(), and assert captured request contains every configured taxonomy label (no omissions, no stale list) at dispatch time.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is response-handling across module boundaries (provider adapter -> normalization/validation -> public return). A deterministic stubbed API response reproduces the failure; real infra is unnecessary. Rejected higher levels: System/Agentic are disqualified by behavior text (`API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy`) because test target is guardrail mapping logic, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Stub provider to return an out-of-taxonomy label, call classify(), and assert returned value is exactly `unknown` and never the raw provider label.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to the local normalization step with fixed input/output determinism; no cross-module interaction is required to expose failure. Rejected higher levels: Integration/System/Agentic are disqualified by `identical raw API responses` and `normalization step`, which describe pure deterministic transformation.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization function repeatedly with identical raw response fixtures (case/whitespace/synonym variants if supported) and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires repeated live model calls and evaluates stability of model-produced labels over time; mocks would hide true nondeterminism/drift. This directly depends on real AI output behavior, so Agentic is the minimum. Rejected higher level: Workflow is disqualified because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-stage agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N live classifications for the same input against the real provider, log raw responses and final labels, and assert label consistency across trials when provider response content is unchanged.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at handoff between tokenizer/budget policy and dispatch path (`before dispatch`). A pure unit test can miss bypasses in wiring; an in-process fake client can verify no over-budget request is sent. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because no real OS/network is needed; Agentic is disqualified because correctness is policy enforcement, not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget, pass oversized document, and assert module applies configured truncate/reject policy and either sends compliant payload or blocks dispatch; verify via captured outbound request.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: Behavior requires a real AI provider call and relies on provider-reported token accounting; stubs cannot catch provider-side counting differences that cause real budget overruns. Under the LLM-dependency rule, live AI API execution path defaults to Agentic unless only local infrastructure is tested; here provider token metrics are the subject. Rejected higher level: Workflow is disqualified because this is a single call budget check, not multi-step agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live classification on representative documents and assert provider-reported usage fields are <= configured budget per call; fail on any overage.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This targets retry orchestration (error signal -> wait/backoff -> retry -> success) across local modules. Deterministic stubbed provider responses plus controllable clock can reproduce failure without real network. Rejected higher levels: System is disqualified because real HTTP/rate-limit infra is not required for this logic; Agentic is disqualified because no model-output-quality decision is under test.
behaviors.test_description: Stub provider to return rate-limit error then success, use fake clock/timer controls, and assert module performs wait/backoff and retries, ultimately returning successful classification instead of surfacing immediate error.
